course.title: Mathematics - Grade 10 (WAEC Syllabus)

course.description:
Master Grade 10 Mathematics with comprehensive coverage of the WAEC syllabus.

📚 Curriculum Coverage:
- Algebra & Equations
- Geometry & Mensuration
- Trigonometry
- Statistics & Probability

🎯 Aligned With:
- WAEC syllabus 2025-2026
- National curriculum standards
- School term structure

✅ Includes:
- Video lessons for every topic

- Progress tracking
- Termly assessments

👥 Perfect For:
- Grade 10 students
- Homeschoolers
- Exam retakers
- Students needing extra support
course._comment_course_type: REQUIRED! This is an ACADEMIC course. Options: 'professional', 'academic', 'exam_prep'
course._comment_academic_fields: Academic-specific fields - school, faculty, class_level must exist in database
course._comment_school: Use school ID or exact name. Example IDs: 'university-of-buea', 'mit', 'harvard-university'
course._comment_faculty: Use faculty ID or exact name. Example IDs: 'faculty-of-science', 'school-of-engineering'
course._comment_class_level: Use class level ID or exact name. Example IDs: 'cm-secondary-form4', 'us-grade-10'
course._comment_course_credits: Number of academic credits (optional, typically for university courses)
course.requirements:
- Completed Grade 9 Mathematics
- Scientific calculator
- Notebook for practice
- Geometry set (compass, protractor, ruler)
course.learning_outcomes:
✓ Master all WAEC Grade 10 Math topics
✓ Solve WAEC-style exam questions
✓ Score 70%+ in school exams
✓ Build strong foundation for Grade 11
✓ Improve problem-solving speed
✓ Understand real-world applications
course.target_audience: Grade 10 students, homeschoolers, students preparing for WAEC, exam retakers

modules.title: Term 1 - Unit 1: Algebraic Expressions
modules.description: Learn to simplify, expand and factorize algebraic expressions. WAEC syllabus topics 1.1-1.3
learning_objectives: Solve linear equations in one variable

lessons.title: Introduction to Algebraic Expressions
lessons.description: What are algebraic expressions and why do we use them?
lessons.content: Learn the fundamentals of algebraic expressions and their real-world applications
learning_objectives: Understand the importance of algebra in mathematics

sections.title: What is an Algebraic Expression?
sections.text_content:
# Algebraic Expressions

An algebraic expression is a mathematical phrase that can contain:
- Numbers (called constants)
- Variables (letters like x, y, a, b)
- Operations (+, -, ×, ÷)

## Examples:
- 3x + 5
- 2a - 4b + 7
- x² + 2xy - y²

## Key Terms:
- **Term**: A single part of the expression (e.g., 3x, 5, -4b)
- **Coefficient**: The number multiplying the variable (e.g., 3 in 3x)
- **Variable**: The letter representing an unknown value (e.g., x, y)
- **Constant**: A number without a variable (e.g., 5, 7)

## Why Learn Algebra?
Algebra helps us:
- Solve real-world problems
- Find unknown values
- Model relationships between quantities
- Prepare for advanced mathematics

sections.title: Visual Explanation
sections.description: Visual guide showing terms, coefficients, and variables

quiz_questions.text: In the expression 7x + 3, what is the coefficient?
quiz_questions.explanation: The coefficient is the number multiplying the variable. In 7x, the coefficient is 7.
Terms are separated by + or - signs. This expression has 3 terms: 4a, -2b, and 5.

resources.title: Algebra Terminology Reference Sheet
resources.description: Definitions of all key algebra terms

lessons.title: Simplifying Algebraic Expressions
lessons.description: Learn to combine like terms and simplify expressions

sections.title: Worked Example 1
sections.text_content:
## Example: Simplify 3x + 5x - 2x

**Step 1:** Identify like terms
- All terms have the variable 'x'
- Coefficients: 3, 5, -2

**Step 2:** Combine the coefficients
3 + 5 - 2 = 6

**Step 3:** Write the simplified expression
6x

**Answer:** 3x + 5x - 2x = 6x

sections.title: Video: Multiple Examples
sections.description: Watch me simplify 5 different expressions step-by-step

resources.description: Real WAEC exam questions on this topic

modules.title: Term 1 - Unit 2: Quadratic Equations
modules.description: Master solving quadratic equations using multiple methods

lessons.title: Introduction to Quadratic Equations
lessons.description: Understanding what makes an equation quadratic

sections.title: Definition and Standard Form
sections.text_content:
# Quadratic Equations

A quadratic equation is an equation of the form:

**ax² + bx + c = 0**

Where:
- a, b, c are constants (numbers)
- a ≠ 0 (if a = 0, it becomes linear)
- x is the variable (unknown)

## Examples:
1. x² - 5x + 6 = 0 (a=1, b=-5, c=6)
2. 2x² + 3x - 2 = 0 (a=2, b=3, c=-2)
3. x² = 16 (which is x² - 16 = 0)

## Why a ≠ 0?
If a = 0, the equation becomes:
0x² + bx + c = 0
bx + c = 0

This is linear, not quadratic!
